records.summary: Banham is a premium, heritage-focused London security brand with a polished editorial homepage emphasising craftsmanship and brand storytelling. The site reads more like a luxury lifestyle destination than an emergency locksmith, with no visible click-to-call, no 24/7 emergency messaging, and no service area callout above the fold.
records.topOpportunity: Add a prominent emergency click-to-call button and 24/7 availability statement above the fold to capture high-intent locked-out mobile users who will otherwise bounce instantly
records.notes: Banham is a mature, well-branded business that has prioritised brand prestige over conversion urgency — a mid-tier opportunity for an agency to pitch emergency UX optimisation and local SEO schema rather than a full rebuild.

records.summary: A clean, consultative landing page for Banham's free in-person security survey, well structured with a clear step-by-step process and FAQ section. However, it relies entirely on a form enquiry flow with no phone number, no click-to-call, and no urgency signals for users with immediate needs.
records.topOpportunity: Introduce a visible phone number or click-to-call CTA alongside the enquiry form to reduce friction for users who want immediate human contact rather than a callback workflow
records.notes: The page is well-designed for considered buyers but lacks any fast-contact pathway, making it a weak conversion point for higher-urgency prospects — a targeted CRO pitch around contact accessibility would resonate here.
